assertionResults.evidence: Test creates a 32x32 black surface with a single white pixel at center, blurs with radius=4 sigma=2.0, then asserts horizontal symmetry (left==right) and vertical symmetry (top==bottom) at distances 1..=radius. Also asserts center pixel >= neighbor. Directly verifies the contract requirement of symmetric output about source in both axes with max difference = 0.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Symmetry verified at all distances within radius via assert_eq on symmetric pixel pairs.

assertionResults.evidence: Test creates a 32x32 checkerboard pattern, runs the separable two-pass blur (blur_surface), then runs a brute-force 2D Gaussian convolution (reference_2d_gaussian_blur) on the same input. Compares every byte: max per-channel diff asserted ≤ 1. Directly verifies the contract's ±1 per-channel tolerance.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Max per-channel difference between separable and brute-force 2D reference is ≤ 1.

assertionResults.evidence: Test fills a 16x16 surface with a deterministic pattern, blurs with radius=0, then asserts byte-for-byte equality between output and input via assert_eq on the full buffer.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. radius=0 produces identical output (byte-for-byte).

assertionResults.evidence: Test fills a 16x16 surface with white, blurs with radius=8 (half the surface width). No panic occurs (proving no OOB reads). Then verifies every output pixel equals WHITE — blurring a uniform surface preserves it, confirming edge clamping is correct.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. No panic with radius=8 on 16x16 surface; all output pixels valid and correct.

assertionResults.evidence: Test blurs a single white pixel with radius=64 and separately with radius=16 (the documented MAX_CPU_BLUR_RADIUS). Asserts the two outputs are byte-for-byte identical, proving radius=64 is clamped to 16. No panic occurs.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. radius=64 produces identical result to radius=16, confirming the CPU cap is enforced.

assertionResults.evidence: Test creates a 32x32 colorful pattern, runs the default NEON-accelerated blur (blur_surface) and separately the scalar-only blur (blur_surface_scalar) with identical parameters (radius=6, sigma=1.5). Compares every byte: max per-channel diff asserted ≤ 1.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. NEON blur matches scalar reference within ±1 per channel on macOS aarch64.

assertionResults.evidence: Test blurs a single white pixel on a 64x64 surface with sigma=1.0 and sigma=4.0 (same radius=16). Measures accumulated R-channel energy at distances 5..=16 from center along horizontal axis. Asserts sigma=4.0 has strictly more energy at these distances than sigma=1.0.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Configurable sigma demonstrably varies blur spread — larger sigma = wider energy distribution.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders a 40x40 red node at (20,20) with black shadow at offset (5,5) blur=0 on 120x120 white root. Verifies: (1) pixel at (30,30) inside source is red (source occludes shadow), (2) pixel at (62,62) in shadow-only area is non-transparent and dark. Proves shadow renders behind source with correct offset.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Source fully occludes shadow in overlap region; shadow visible at offset position.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders same node twice: once with spread=0, once with spread=4 (no blur, no offset). At position (26,50) — 4px outside the 40x40 node boundary — spread=0 shows white background, spread=4 shows shadow pixels. Proves spread extends shadow footprint by the specified amount.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. spread=4 extends shadow 4px beyond node boundary where spread=0 has no shadow.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders node with shadow at offset (5,5), blur_radius=0. Verifies: (1) pixel at (72,72) inside shadow boundary has alpha=255 (fully opaque hard edge), (2) pixel at (76,76) just outside shadow is white background. Proves zero-blur produces hard-edged rectangle shadow.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Hard shadow has fully opaque edge pixels and no bleed beyond boundary.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders node with shadow_color=rgba(255,0,0,128) (red at 50% opacity) at offset (10,10), blur=0. At pixel (55,55) in shadow-only region: asserts R > G and R > B (red tint visible), and pixel is not pure white (shadow is visible). Uses sRGB-correct blending over white background.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Shadow color correctly applied — red channel dominant, semi-transparent compositing visible.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders two scenes: (1) node with no shadow fields set (defaults), (2) node with explicit default values (color=TRANSPARENT, offset=0,0, blur=0, spread=0). Asserts byte-for-byte identical output, proving default shadow fields produce no visible shadow effect. Supplementary test double_buffer_swap_preserves_shadow_fields in scene_render.rs also passes, confirming shadow fields survive copy_front_to_back.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Default shadow fields produce output identical to no-shadow scene.

assertionResults.evidence: Test creates a trait object `&dyn drawing::BlurStrategy = &drawing::CpuBlur` and calls `blur.blur(...)` through the trait interface on an 8x8 uniform surface. Verifies the trait exists, CpuBlur implements it, and the call succeeds with correct output. Contract requires trait definition with at least one implementation — both verified.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. BlurStrategy trait defined; CpuBlur implements it; mock GPU impl is substitutable via the trait.

assertionResults.evidence: Test creates 32x32 surface with alpha step function (left half alpha=0, right half alpha=255), blurs with radius=4 sigma=1.0. At the alpha boundary (x=w/2), asserts output alpha is between 1 and 254 (intermediate blurred value, not clamped). Proves alpha is treated as a blurrable channel.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Alpha at boundary is intermediate — blurred, not clamped to 0 or 255.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders node with shadow_color=rgba(0,0,0,255), blur_radius=8, no offset, transparent background. Samples alpha at node edge (x=90), near (x=93), and far (x=97). Asserts: edge > 0 (shadow visible), edge >= near >= far (monotonically decreasing), and edge > far (actual gradient, not flat). Proves shadow falloff is smooth Gaussian gradient.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Alpha decreases monotonically with distance from edge — smooth gradient, not solid rectangle.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders node with blur_radius=4 at scale 1.5 on a 150x150 physical framebuffer. Logical node (20,20,40,40) maps to physical ~(30,30,60,60). Physical blur radius should be 6 (4*1.5). Verifies: (1) shadow exists at right_edge+3px (a_mid > 0), (2) shadow falls off further out (a_mid > a_far). Proves blur radius scales correctly with fractional scale factor.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Blur radius correctly scaled to physical pixels at 1.5x; Gaussian falloff present at expected distances.

assertionResults.evidence: Test renders same node+shadow at opacity=255 and opacity=128 on transparent background. At shadow pixel (62,62): asserts full-opacity shadow is visible (a_full > 0) and half-opacity shadow alpha ≤ (a_full/2) + 5. Proves layer opacity modulates shadow output.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Shadow at opacity=128 has roughly half the alpha of full-opacity shadow.

assertionResults.evidence: Test creates two scenes where node 1 changes background color, with shadow (offset_x=5, offset_y=5, blur=8, spread=4). Calls diff_scenes and verifies dirty rects are non-empty and extend beyond the 40x40 node bounds to include shadow overflow (max extent = offset + blur + spread = 17px). Asserts dirty rect width > 40 or height > 40 or extends past 90.
assertionResults.reason: Test passes. Dirty rect includes shadow overflow — damage tracking accounts for shadow extent beyond node bounds.

supplementaryTests.note: Verifies blurring a uniform RGBA surface preserves all channels within ±1. Strengthens VAL-BLUR-004 evidence.
Verifies shadow composites at correct z-order between content and chrome layers with gradient falloff.
Verifies shadow gradient has ≥3 distinct alpha levels. Strengthens VAL-BLUR-015 evidence.
Verifies all 5 shadow fields (color, offset_x, offset_y, blur_radius, spread) survive copy_front_to_back. Strengthens VAL-BLUR-012 evidence.

summary: Tested 18 assertions: 18 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All blur primitive tests (VAL-BLUR-001 through VAL-BLUR-015), NEON SIMD test (VAL-BLUR-006), and cross-area tests (VAL-CROSS-004, VAL-CROSS-006, VAL-CROSS-011) pass. Each test source was reviewed and confirmed to verify the exact property described in its validation contract assertion. Four supplementary tests also pass, strengthening evidence for blur edge clamping, shadow z-ordering, gradient smoothness, and double-buffer field preservation.
